description: The status tracker investigation revealed that tests across the codebase use mocks that hide real bugs. Executor tests use MockHandler, integration tests use mock backends, and no tests verify state persistence end-to-end. Audit all test files and fix gaps: replace mocks with real backends where feasible, add state persistence assertions, test checkpoint resume, test concurrent registry access, test error recovery/rollback.
title: Audit test suite for mock-hiding-real-bugs patterns

description: When creating a worktree with git worktree add, the beads post-checkout hook runs and fails if the Dolt database name doesnt match (e.g. database from a previous worktree prefix). This causes git worktree add to return non-zero even though the worktree was successfully created. superintendent sees the non-zero exit and reports failure. Need to investigate: (1) should the post-checkout hook be more resilient to missing databases (2) should superintendent ignore hook failures during worktree creation (3) should worktree creation use core.hooksPath=/dev/null
title: Investigate beads post-checkout hook failing git worktree add

description: superintendent run should reuse existing worktrees instead of always creating new ones. Current behavior: run always calls git worktree add -b which fails if branch/worktree exists. resume exists as a separate command but doesn't spawn an agent. Proposed behavior for run: (1) worktree + branch exist → skip creation, spawn agent (2) branch exists but no worktree → git worktree add <path> <branch> without -b (3) neither exists → current behavior with -b. The resume command can become a thin convenience wrapper or be removed. The --force flag should handle nuke-and-recreate.
title: Unify run and resume: reuse existing worktrees

description: Detect package managers and heavy dependencies from repo files (pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, package.json), pre-install system packages in the sandbox template image to avoid slow pip/npm installs during agent startup. Currently the template is hardcoded (Dolt + beads CLI). Need to extend _handle_prepare_template to inspect the repo and add apt-get installs for known heavy deps (GDAL, pyfarmhash, weasyprint, etc.).
title: Smarter template builds: repo-aware Dockerfile generation

close_reason: All three changes implemented and tested

description: Review every use of # noqa in src/ and tests/. For each one, determine whether it's hiding a real issue (like the ARG002 in DryRunDockerBackend that was suppressing an actual bug) or is a legitimate suppression. Remove any that are papering over problems and fix the underlying code instead.
title: Audit all noqa suppressions in codebase

description: Three fixes from design spec: (1) register entries in run() after successful execution, (2) sandbox liveness check in check_agent_status(), (3) tests for registration, liveness, and round-trip
title: Implement status tracker fix (registration, liveness, tests)

description:
Beads has first-class worktree support via a .beads/redirect file. When bd finds .beads/redirect, it follows the path inside to the real .beads/ directory, sharing the Dolt server and database.

Implementation: After git worktree add succeeds in _handle_create_worktree (step_handler.py), create the redirect:

    main_beads = repo_path / '.beads'
    if main_beads.is_dir():
        wt_beads = worktree_path / '.beads'
        wt_beads.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        (wt_beads / 'redirect').write_text(str(main_beads) + '\n')

Key findings from research:
- .beads/redirect supports absolute or relative paths, single-level only
- The file is .gitignored by beads (paths would be wrong in other clones)
- bd worktree create does this natively, but superintendent manages its own worktrees
- All worktrees connect to the same Dolt server instance (port stored in main .beads/dolt-server.port)
- BEADS_DIR env var is an alternative but redirect file is preferred (persists in filesystem, bd doctor validates it, bd where shows chain)
- No change needed for sandbox/container targets — they correctly run isolated Dolt servers
- bd dolt push/pull exists for remote sync but is unnecessary when sharing via redirect

Depends on superintendent-760 (run/resume unification) since worktree creation logic will change there.
title: Share beads database across worktrees via redirect file

close_reason: All three changes implemented and tested

title: Token permission introspection via GitHub API
